stats.label: Strength
stats.description:
It measures **physical power** and **muscle**, governing both the capacity to **lift** and **carry heavy loads** and **the force behind melee strikes**.

*Some example actions who use this are:*
- Athletics
- Melee attacks
- Breaking objects

stats.label: Dexterity
stats.description:
It measures **agility**, **reflexes**, and **balance**, encompassing both **fine motor skills** and **gross motor coordination**.

*Some example actions who use this are:*
- Acrobatics
- Stealth
- Sleight of hand

stats.label: Constitution
stats.description:
It measures **endurance** and **bodily resilience**, governing the ability to **withstand physical punishment**, **resist disease**, and **push beyond normal limits**.

*Some example actions who use this are:*
- Holding breath
- Surviving harsh environments
- Resisting poison or disease

stats.label: Intelligence
stats.description:
It measures **reasoning**, **memory**, and **analytical thinking**, governing the capacity to **recall knowledge**, **solve problems**, and **understand complex systems**.

*Some example actions who use this are:*
- Arcana & History
- Investigation
- Identifying magical items

stats.label: Wisdom
stats.description:
It measures **perception**, **intuition**, and **willpower**, **governing awareness of the world** and the ability to **read situations** and **resist mental influence**.

*Some example actions who use this are:*
- Perception & Insight
- Survival
- Medicine

stats.label: Charisma
stats.description:
It measures **force of personality**, **persuasiveness**, and **social presence**, governing the ability to **influence**, **deceive**, or **inspire** others.

*Some example actions who use this are:*
- Persuasion & Deception
- Intimidation
- Performance

classes.name: Barbarian
classes.description: A **warrior powered by rage** that unlocks hisood at **carrying heavy stuff**, **destroying hard stuff** and **generating fear** on his en true capacities, mainly a active user of **brute force** developed by his **low usage of reason**, gemies.

classes.name: Rogue
classes.description: A stealth agent, dedicated to being agile, not so strong but having a great ability of hiding in plain view, silent, agile and efficient.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.description: It determines your ability to stay balanced, navigate tricky terrain, and perform physical stunts

traits.description: You gain **Heroic Inspiration** whenever you **finish a Long Rest**.
traits.name: Resourceful

traits.description: You gain **proficiency** in **one skill** of your choice.
traits.name: Skillful

traits.description: You **gain** an **Origin feat** of your choice.
traits.name: Versatile

traits.description: You have **Darkvision** with a range of **{{distance}} feet**.
traits.name: Darkvision

traits.description: You have **Resistance to Poison damage**. You also have **Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Poisoned condition**.
traits.name: Dwarven Resilience

traits.description: Your **Hit Point maximum increases by 1**, and **it increases by 1** again **whenever you gain a level**.
traits.name: Dwarven Toughness

traits.description:
As a Bonus Action, you gain **Tremorsense** with a range of **{{distance}} feet** for **{{time}} minutes**. You **must be on a stone surface or touching a stone surface to use this Tremorsense**. The stone can be natural or worked.

**You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus**, and you regain all expended uses **when you finish a Long Rest**.
traits.name: Stonecunning

species.description:
Found throughout the multiverse, humans are as varied as they are numerous, and they endeavor to achieve as much as they can in the years they are given. Their ambition and resourcefulness are commended, respected, and feared on many worlds.

Humans are as diverse in appearance as the people of Earth, and they have many gods. Scholars dispute the origin of humanity, but one of the earliest known human gatherings is said to have occurred in Sigil, the torus-shaped city at the center of the multiverse and the place where the Common language was born. From there, humans could have spread to every part of the multiverse, bringing the City of Doors’ cosmopolitanism with them.
species.name: Human

species.description:
Dwarves were raised from the earth in the elder days by a deity of the forge. Called by various names on different worlds—Moradin, Reorx, and others—that god gave dwarves an affinity for stone and metal and for living underground. The god also made them resilient like the mountains, with a life span of about 350 years.

Squat and often bearded, the original dwarves carved cities and strongholds into mountainsides and under the earth. Their oldest legends tell of conflicts with the monsters of mountaintops and the Underdark, whether those monsters were towering giants or subterranean horrors. Inspired by those tales, dwarves of any culture often sing of valorous deeds—especially of the little overcoming the mighty.

On some worlds in the multiverse, the first settlements of dwarves were built in hills or mountains, and the families who trace their ancestry to those settlements call themselves hill dwarves or mountain dwarves, respectively. The Greyhawk and Dragonlance settings have such communities.
species.name: Dwarf

rules.description:
If you **(a player character)** have **Heroic Inspiration**, you can **expend it to reroll any die immediately after rolling it**, and **you must use the new roll**.

If you gain Heroic Inspiration **but already have it**, it’s lost unless you give it to a player character who lacks it."
rules.name: Heroic Inspiration

rules.description:
While you have the **Incapacitated** condition, you experience the following effects.

- **Inactive**: You **can’t take any action**, **Bonus Action**, or **Reaction**.

- **No Concentration**: Your Concentration is broken.

- **Speechless**: You can’t speak.

- **Surprised**: If you’re Incapacitated **when you roll Initiative, you have Disadvantage on the roll**.
rules.name: Incapacitated
